sourceText: Expresses the belief that true strength comes not from military or political might, but from moral and spiritual truth.
He who comes to us with a sword, shall die by the sword.
A resolute warning to any who would invade the lands of Rus', showing a determination to meet aggression with force.
There is no greater love than to lay down one's life for one's country.
Highlights the value of ultimate sacrifice for the protection of one's land and people, signifying patriotism and duty.

Pushkin endured years of exile due to censorship and political persecution by the Tsarist government. He also struggled with social scandals, constant financial difficulties, and the pressures surrounding his wife's honor.

He transformed his isolated exile into his most creative period, delving into Russian folklore and history. This allowed him to forge a uniquely Russian literary voice and sublimate adversity into art, completing masterpieces like 'Eugene Onegin'.
Deception can be raised to a virtue, and truth can be spoken in such a way that it is a lie.
This means that the presentation and intent behind words matter more than their literal truth.
The soul of another is a dark forest.
This implies that it is impossible to truly know another person's inner thoughts and motivations.
Habit is a second nature, and it is given to us from above.
This suggests that our routines and habits shape our character and destiny, almost as if by divine decree.
Inherited a crumbling empire beset by external invasions from Normans, Pechenegs, and Seljuk Turks, alongside internal political instability and a depleted treasury.
